skill.name: docs-agent
skill.description: Maintains README files, agent documentation, API references, architecture notes, and changelogs for Unite-Hub projects.
skill.summary: Keeps Unite-Hub project documentation aligned with code, APIs, architecture, agents, and release changes.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many alerts, but review shows they are mainly Markdown fences, inline filenames, and documentation examples. The skill does guide broad local documentation discovery and includes a local fetch example, so publication risk is low rather than safe. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious external network destinations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Trigger External Command Alerts
low_findings.description: The external command findings are documentation examples, not executable skill code. The notable commands locate Markdown files and run a future docs check, which are legitimate documentation tasks but can inspect broad project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside Markdown code fences and support the declared documentation workflow. They are still recorded as low risk because an agent following them may enumerate project files.

low_findings.title: Local API Fetch Example Is Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The network finding is an example request to a relative API path. It does not send secrets to an external host or define hidden telemetry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch target is a local relative endpoint used in API documentation. No external domain or secret-bearing payload appears in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-crypto alerts match Markdown filenames, headings, or prose such as .md and Description. The reconnaissance alerts match benign documentation discovery examples and diagram syntax, not stealthy host profiling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review found no cryptographic API usage, hashing implementation, host fingerprinting, or hidden system inventory logic. The matched text is documentation content.

content.value_statement: Project documentation often drifts after code, API, and workflow changes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to update docs consistently.
actual_capabilities: Reviews existing Markdown documentation before proposing updates.
Updates README sections for features, installation, usage, deployment, and contributing notes.
Documents API endpoints with methods, request details, responses, errors, and examples.
Maintains agent definitions and system notes for Claude workflows.
Creates changelog entries using standard Added, Changed, Fixed, and Security categories.
Drafts text-based and Mermaid architecture diagrams for system changes.
limitations: Targets Unite-Hub documentation patterns and may need adaptation for other repositories.
Does not generate an interactive documentation site or API explorer.
Requires accurate change context from code review or user input.
Example commands may inspect local project files when followed by an agent.

use_cases.title: Refresh README After A Feature Release
use_cases.description: Update feature lists, setup steps, usage notes, and deployment guidance after a product change.

use_cases.title: Document New API Routes
use_cases.description: Turn route behavior into clear endpoint documentation with request, response, authentication, and error details.

use_cases.title: Synchronize Agent And Architecture Notes
use_cases.description: Keep Claude agent definitions, system status, architecture diagrams, and workflow notes aligned with implementation changes.

prompt_templates.title: Update The README
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the README for the recent feature changes. Preserve the current structure and keep the tone concise.

prompt_templates.title: Document An API Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Document the new API endpoint. Include method, path, authentication, request fields, response shape, errors, and a short usage example.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Changelog Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog entry for these changes. Group items under Added, Changed, Fixed, and Security when relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Architecture And Agent Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the recent refactor and update architecture notes, agent definitions, data flow details, and affected README sections.

output_examples.input: Document a new bulk contact update endpoint.
output_examples.output: A concise API section with purpose, authentication, request fields, success response, common errors, and a client usage note.

output_examples.input: Sync docs after adding a dashboard analytics page.
output_examples.output: Updated README feature text, architecture notes for the new page, and a changelog entry under Added.

output_examples.input: Refresh agent documentation after adding a new workflow agent.
output_examples.output: A new agent section with role, capabilities, tools, invocation guidance, and integration notes.

best_practices: Provide the changed files or a clear summary before asking for documentation updates.
Review generated examples for accuracy before publishing them.
Update changelog and architecture notes during the same release cycle as code changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent API behavior without route or schema context.
Do not publish generated docs before checking links, examples, and version numbers.
Do not use it as a general code security reviewer.

faq.question: What documentation does this skill maintain?
faq.answer: It focuses on README files, Claude agent docs, API references, architecture notes, deployment guides, and changelogs.

faq.question: Can it document new API endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline methods, paths, authentication, request fields, responses, errors, and short usage examples.

faq.question: Does it work outside Unite-Hub?
faq.answer: It can be adapted, but its examples and file names are tailored to Unite-Hub documentation conventions.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides documentation guidance and example commands for an agent to follow when appropriate.

faq.question: Why is the security rating low risk?
faq.answer: The reviewed alerts are mostly Markdown examples, but the skill may guide broad local file discovery for documentation work.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
